mission.title: dapr: [PubSub] override cloud event attributes via metadata
mission.description: [PubSub] override cloud event attributes via metadata. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current dapr deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation.

steps.title: Review dapr configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
## Describe the feature
At present, there are two ways to publish a Cloud Event via PubSub

1. Supply a payload, which is then automatically wrapped by Dapr in a Cloud Event envelope.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [PubSub] override cloud event attributes via metadata
steps.description:
# Description

Allows overriding certain cloud event property via metadata / headers in pubsub publishing. The `cloudevent-` prefix must be used for the metadata key. For example `cloudevent-source` would manually override the `source` value.
```yaml
Note the `Content-Type: application/cloudevents+json` header

We could add a new Content-Type header such as `application/x-dapr-cloudevent` where we don't require a full cloud event envelope. Such as:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[PubSub] override cloud event attributes via metadata" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
# Description

Allows overriding certain cloud event property via metadata / headers in pubsub publishing. The `cloudevent-` prefix must be used for the metadata key. For example `cloudevent-source` would manually override the `source` value.

Note the `Content-Type: application/cloudevents+json` header

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dapr installed or the issue environment reproducible.
